auth_notes: Most modern Property Management Systems (PMS) utilize Header-based API Keys or OAuth2. Legacy hospitality systems may require SOAP/XML-based authentication or IP whitelisting.
rate_limits: Varies by provider; typically 60-120 requests per minute per property.
gotchas: Many industry-standard PMS APIs still use XML/SOAP, requiring complex body construction in n8n
Webhooks often only send an ID, requiring a secondary 'Fetch Details' HTTP request
Strict rate limits can lead to 429 errors during high-volume check-in periods
client_must_provide: API Documentation for the specific vendor
research_notes: As 'PMS' is a category, the integration will require the HTTP Request node. The developer must first determine if the API is RESTful or SOAP-based. If REST, use JSON in the body; if SOAP, use the 'RAW' body type with XML. Webhooks will require a dedicated Webhook node and potentially a static IP if the PMS provider requires whitelisting.

effort_recommendation.rationale: As 'PMS' is a category, the integration will require the HTTP Request node. The developer must first determine if the API is RESTful or SOAP-based. If REST, use JSON in the body; if SOAP, use the 'RAW' body type with XML. Webhooks will require a dedicated Webhook node and potentially a static IP if the PMS provider requires whitelisting.
